old_claim.note: Quantum Computing is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim_absent: No direct Metaverse Deep Immersion to Quantum Computing edge remains because virtual-world sources emphasize low-latency networks, cloud-edge/HPC computing, XR, data, AI, cybersecurity, HCI, and media standards rather than quantum computing as a required runtime capability.

source_shape.source_locator: The European Commission Rolling Plan says virtual worlds require coordinated integration of low-latency networks, high-performance computing/storage, VR/AR, data and AI, blockchain/NFT, cybersecurity, HCI, media, and interoperability building blocks.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source-backed virtual-world infrastructure stack uses networks, cloud-edge or high-performance computing, XR, data, and AI, not quantum computing as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Quantum technologies may appear as an adjacent standards topic, but the source does not make quantum computing an enabling or required technology for immersive virtual worlds.

ontology_before: The graph implied quantum computing was a prerequisite for broad deep-immersion metaverse systems.
ontology_after: The graph removes the unsupported quantum-computing dependency and keeps concrete digital-platform and interface dependencies.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct metaverse_deep_immersion -> quantum_computing edge removed and locked absent in 2026-06-24-metaverse-deep-immersion-scope.json.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped to a quantum-simulated virtual world architecture.
A source shows quantum computing is required to run or network immersive virtual worlds rather than an adjacent research area.
A downstream node represents quantum-generated simulation environments and should point separately to quantum_computing.
short_reason: Virtual-world sources support networked XR and cloud-edge/HPC infrastructure, not quantum computing as a prerequisite.
